title: Lab Officer/ Research Engineer (FAB Operation)
description:
Job Description

Be part of an exciting FAB Operations Team working on cutting-edge process technologies.

Job Responsibilities:

  • Looking for reliable individual - Operate multiple type of semiconductor equipment in cleanroom environment.
  • Ability to use computer-based programs and applications to perform daily work/duties.
  • Operate equipment based on the required process specifications (e.g. procedures, change notifications, etc.).
  • Participate actively and be responsible in assigned on-the-job assignments and other required trainings when requested. Provide machine operations coverage and backup supports when necessary.
  • Adequate understanding of the Equipment in-charge, monitor performance, timely communication with Engineers-in-charge to perform equipment errors recovery promptly when necessary.
  • Demonstrate excellent teamwork to promptly execute process sequences and data collections if required.
  • Always follow safety protocols & rules in the Clean Room.
  • Ensure 6S in the work environment and promptly report hazards/ suspicious/ abnormal activities or personnel if any identified.
  • Address potential areas for improvements such as wafers processing cycle time, Equipment utilization, Safety/ Compliances, and cost savings.

Job Requirements:

  • Diploma/Degree in MicroElectronics/Electrical/Mechanical/Chemical Engineering or equivalent.
  • Understanding on semiconductor process - at least 1-3 years of experience in semiconductor processes (Photolithography, Dry/Wet Etch/Cleans, ThinFilms, Diffusions, Chemical mechanical Polishings, Metrology etc.)
  • Must be a self-starter, dependable and highly motivated.

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

    e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions. e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives.
